notes: Names 6 products with pricing and latency data
Optimization: constrained researcher agent to max 2 tool calls (was unbounded, avg 13.7).
Average time dropped from 146.6s to 20.4s — a 7.2x speedup, well under the 60s target.
Quality maintained: all 9 assertions pass (5 original + 4 new discriminating assertions).
New assertions now discriminate: iteration-1 would FAIL 'completes-under-60s' on all 3 evals.
Citation quality maintained: 4-5 sourced URLs per eval with markdown Sources section.
Token usage comparable (~29.9K vs ~28.8K) — the agent produces similar depth with fewer tool calls.
The key insight: haiku was over-searching. Constraining to 1-2 calls forces it to synthesize better from the first search, rather than doing many incremental searches.
